details: The agent ran comprehensive kubectl commands against the cluster and correctly identified all 11 fault namespaces with accurate root causes: (1) fault-configmap: missing ConfigMap 'app-config'; (2) fault-crashloop: database connection refused at db.internal:5432; (3) fault-endpoints: service selector mismatch (app=web-backend-v2 vs app=web-backend on pods); (4) fault-imagepull: non-existent image tag nginx:1.99.99-nonexistent; (5) fault-init: init container crashing due to migration table lock; (6) fault-multicontainer: log-shipper sidecar cannot resolve DNS for log-collector.logging.svc; (7) fault-oom: container OOMKilled with 25Mi memory limit; (8) fault-pvc: StorageClass 'fast-ssd' not found; (9) fault-readiness: readiness probe checking wrong port 8080; (10) fault-rollout: stalled rollout with invalid image nginx:2.0-does-not-exist; (11) fault-unschedulable: node selector disktype=ssd-nvme-gen5 matches no nodes. The healthy namespace was not flagged. All diagnoses are supported by actual command output.
